items.title: Subcomponents of reward processing in adolescent anhedonia
items.author: Ciara McCabe
items.content: Understanding the link between anhedonia and reward processing in adolescence can enhance mental health interventions specifically for young people. In this Review, Ma et al. examine the evidence on consummatory pleasure, anticipation, motivation and learning, and discuss real-time assessment of anhedonia and the reward cycle.

items.title: Young children are sensitive to the limitations of hearsay
items.author: Teresa Schubert
items.content: Young children are sensitive to the limitations of hearsay

items.title: A language processing time window of around three seconds
items.author: Xinchi Yu
items.content: A language processing time window of around three seconds

items.title: The psychological consequences of mental health awareness efforts
items.author: Carolina Guzman Holst
items.content: Mental health awareness campaigns aim to decrease stigma, increase help-seeking and improve mental health literacy. However, they might also negatively impact how individuals interpret, label and respond to mental health problems. In this Review, Foulkes et al. summarize evidence for the positive and negative psychological impacts of mental health awareness efforts.

items.title: Patterns of healthcare access after cancer
items.author: Maja Furlan de Brito
items.content: Patterns of healthcare access after cancer

items.title: Towards a habit-rupture model of intergroup contact in everyday settings
items.author: Jake Harwood
items.content: The literature assumes that intergroup contact is naturally occurring, positive and consistently associated with positive outcomes, but these premises are inconsistent with everyday intergroup contact experiences. In this Perspective, Paolini et al. propose that variations in contact valence and environmental affordances for self-selection influence segregation dynamics, leading to stable trajectories of contact and intergroup bias.

items.title: Influences of spoken and signed language on numerical cognition
items.author: D. B. Berch
items.content: How numerical information is expressed in language shapes the mental processes that underlie numerical reasoning. In this Review, Berteletti and colleagues compare the impacts of spoken and signed languages on numerical cognition and detail how future work with signed languages can enrich understanding of these impacts.

items.title: Advancing implementation of behavioural medicine in cardiovascular care
items.author: Christopher A. Crawford
items.content: Behavioural and psychosocial interventions can effectively reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. In this Perspective, Burg et al. identify the elements and actions needed for a broad implementation of these interventions in standard cardiovascular care.
